name: Le Loi
description: Le Loi, also known as Emperor Le Thai To, is a pivotal figure in Vietnamese history and mythology. Born in Thanh Hoa province, he rose to prominence in the early 15th century during the Ming Dynasty's occupation of Dai Viet (Vietnam). Witnessing the suffering of his people under oppressive foreign rule, Le Loi rallied a resistance movement known as the Lam Son Uprising. With brilliant military strategy and unwavering determination, he led his forces through numerous battles, employing guerilla tactics and inspiring widespread popular support. The uprising culminated in a decisive victory against the Ming forces at the Battle of Tot Dong - Ngoc Hoi in 1426, effectively ending the occupation. In 1428, Le Loi declared independence and established the Later Le Dynasty, ushering in a new era of Vietnamese sovereignty and prosperity. He implemented significant reforms in government, agriculture, and education, laying the foundation for a strong and independent nation. Le Loi's legacy extends beyond his military achievements; he is revered as a national hero, a symbol of Vietnamese patriotism, and a wise and benevolent ruler who fought for the freedom and well-being of his people. The legend of the Thuan Thien sword, granted to him by the Dragon King, further solidifies his place in Vietnamese folklore, representing divine favor and the righteous struggle against oppression. His reign marked a golden age for Vietnam, solidifying its identity and independence for centuries to come. Le Loi’s impact on Vietnamese culture is immeasurable, influencing art, literature, and national identity.
subtitle: Liberator of Dai Viet, First Le Emperor

keyMyths.title: The Thuan Thien Sword
keyMyths.description: While fishing, Le Loi found a sword blade. Later, he discovered a sword hilt with the inscription 'Thuan Thien.' When combined, the sword radiated light and symbolized his divine mandate to rule.
keyMyths.source: Dai Viet Su Ky Toan Thu

keyMyths.title: The Golden Turtle God
keyMyths.description: After the war, while boating on Luc Thuy Lake (now Hoan Kiem Lake in Hanoi), a golden turtle god emerged and reclaimed the Thuan Thien sword, returning it to the Dragon King and signifying the end of Le Loi's divine mission.

keyMyths.title: The Lam Son Oath
keyMyths.description: Before launching the uprising, Le Loi and his followers swore an oath at Lam Son, pledging their lives to liberate Dai Viet from the Ming. This oath solidified their commitment and served as a rallying cry for the resistance.

sources.author: Ngo Si Lien
sources.work: Dai Viet Su Ky Toan Thu
sources.description: The Complete Annals of Dai Viet, a comprehensive historical record of Vietnam.
sources.text: The Complete Annals of Dai Viet, a comprehensive historical record of Vietnam.
